text.title: My Solo Trip to Germany
text.u: we've all heard of the brothers grim
fairy tales snow white sleeping beauty
hansel and gretel and those stories were
all set in the mysterious enchanting
black forest which is where i am today
hello from germany
[Music]
it's my first day in germany it's my
first time in germany i'm so excited to
be here i'm here with germany tourism
and they've set up a really cool rail
itinerary for me for the week it's going
to be amazing today i'm in the beautiful
city of heidelberg which i believe is
germany's oldest university town and
this morning i've made my way up to the
heidelberg palace or the heidelberg
castle the ruins here are world famous
and i couldn't have asked for a better
day of weather it is blue skies it's
sunny the colors are just starting to
turn in the trees for fall
[Music]
they have lovers locks
on the bridge
i'm spending the afternoon walking
around altstadt i think it's pronounced
it's like old town heidelberg and it's
so cool if you do a trip through germany
you have to stop here there are tons of
restaurants and shops and like narrow
alleyways it's really really neat i'm
surprised i had never even heard of this
place before this trip because it's
definitely an it spot to go to
[Music]
today i'm in the town of bodenboden it's
so cute you guys and look at the view
from my balcony this hotel is adorable
and the streets are so pretty wikipedia
defines bodenboden as a spa town and i
have to say that is my kind of town it's
in southwestern germany almost right on
the border of france and it's known for
its thermal baths which is actually the
first thing that i'm headed to go do
today i'm going to the karakala therm
it's supposed to be amazing they have
bathing pools and i believe they also do
massages there they have saunas
i just got out of the caracala and i had
no idea what i was doing in there so
first i got in line to pay to go in and
about halfway through the line i looked
around and realized everybody had a
sheet of paper in their hands except for
me i had no idea where the paper came
from by the time i got up to pay i must
have looked like a deer in the
headlights because i walked up and i
said hi it's my first time here and the
guy responded it's okay it's not scary
you don't need to be worried so i pay to
go in the man directs me back toward
where the locker area is as i'm walking
back the first image that i see is a
photo of a completely naked man and
woman sitting in the therm
chatting and i stop and i'm like
is this not what i thought it was gonna
be oh also there were these paper signs
up everywhere that showed like a man and
a woman in swimwear and then like a
warning or something on it i couldn't
tell what it meant because it was in
german so i didn't know if it was saying
don't wear swimwear or must wear
swimwear if i have misread this and i'm
about to walk out into
a thermal bath of like 200 naked
strangers
[Applause]
[Music]
but yeah that's just one of the things
about travel it can be very humbling
when you're in another country and you
don't know what you're doing
[Music]
[Applause]
[Music]
[Applause]
[Music]
it's amazing this morning i'm nestled in
the hills of the beautiful black forest
this place i can already feel is so
special you might recognize it from all
the brothers grim fairy tales that were
set here and i don't know if it's a
placebo effect just because i know this
is like a fairy tale spot but it does
feel
kind of magical kind of enchanting i
don't know and if i was going to lead a
couple of kids into the forest with
nothing but breadcrumbs
yeah this is probably a good spot to do
it today's itinerary is really exciting
i'm getting on a bus and traveling to
baudevildbod i believe it's pronounced
it's spelled bad wild bad
which is probably the coolest city name
ever i'm going to be meeting a guide
there and we're going to do a treetop
walk and see a suspension bridge and
just soak in the black forest some more
so it should be really cool
[Music]
so you can just grab the trees and move
them
oh my gosh
that is crazy
yeah and you would never be able to move
it like that on when you're on the floor
right if you're that high then it's
still going like it just sways and sways
that is so crazy
[Music]
this tower has a spiral
wooden walkway all the way up as you can
see but this metal spiral in the middle
is a slide
you get to slide
down on
[Music]
we're doing the slide
okay pull yourself forward lay back and
enjoy here we go
will i push you forward a little bit yes
okay here we go okay thank you
[Music]
so
[Music]
i'm headed up this beautiful
path through the forest to hambach
castle here in
neustadt i have probably pronounced a
lot of things wrong in this video this
is wine country so there's vineyards
everywhere and the streets are so pretty
and the buildings are colorful and it's
so lush and green and sunny and i had
never imagined german wine country
before but this is beautiful like this
view that i'm looking at right now is
stunning
[Music]
[Music]
i just
unintentionally walked down the entire
hill from the castle through the forest
google maps did me so dirty it told me
there was a walking route with like the
little blue dots all the way down the
hill so i went it was not there and i
ended up just kind of stuck in the woods
not on a trail carving down a hill then
i did come upon a path and i stayed on
it hoping that it was the right one and
now i'm amongst a whole bunch of
vineyards
and it's really really beautiful
[Music]
[Applause]
i just cannot get over this grapes on a
vine
castle
what also the path there was a public
walking path i wasn't like actually in
the middle of someone's private property
which is really nice that they put a
public path through the vineyard areas
[Music]
i've made it to frankfurt i've just
checked into my hotel and today is my
last day in germany which is really sad
huge thank you to germany national
tourism for putting together an awesome
itinerary and having me for the week i
could not have come up with this
itinerary on my own and the cool thing
about traveling in germany and why i was
able to see so many things in a week is
because of the deutsche ban rail system
that is amazing and why can't we have
something like this in the us it is so
easy to just hop on a train and zip all
around the country and see the things
you want to see it's more efficient it's
a more sustainable way to travel you can
cover more ground in a shorter amount of
time and i like riding on the train
because then you can watch the
countryside out of the window like you
see even more when you travel by rail
thanks for coming along with me on this
one i hope i've given you some ideas of
things to do if you come to germany and
i will see you guys in the next one bye
[Music]
[Music]
you
